role: A module-scope external React-readable store that mirrors backend registries and controller-installed action handles for components rendered inside tldraw that cannot reach Hud-level React context.
capabilities: Typed RegistryState slice definitions (OINFO, MODEL_OPTIONS, UI_INFO, NODE_STATES, COGNITION_INFO)
Partial patch updates to registry state
Imperative getters for non-React call sites
Module-scope CONNECT action handle for committing connections
Module-scope DRAG_CONN transient drag-to-connect gesture state
Module-scope FORCE_RUN action handle for per-node rerun
substance_why: It implements a complete, dependency-free external store with typed state, subscription/snapshot API, patch updates, imperative getters, and controller hook handles, replacing six mutable globals.
interactions_present: Drag-to-connect gesture state sharing via DRAG_CONN
Connection commit callback slot via CONNECT
interactions_missing: No pointer event handlers are implemented here (only state/hook slots)
CONNECT and FORCE_RUN default to no-op stubs until externally installed
No validation, error handling, or loading state for registry fetches
No selector API, so subscribers cannot subscribe to a single slice
performance_risks: Every set() emits to all listeners even if the relevant slice is unchanged
No selectors/batching, so any registry update re-renders every subscriber
Shallow merge creates a new top-level state object on every patch, defeating snapshot identity/memoization
DRAG_CONN updates may cause store-wide re-renders if subscribed
Large backend objects (OINFO/UI_INFO) are held in module scope and returned by reference, risking accidental mutation
reuse_why: The 'external registry mirror reachable from tldraw' concept and dependency-free store must survive, but the implementation needs typed slices, selectors, batching, and separation of gesture/action handles.
decomposition_hints: Split backend registry slices (OINFO, MODEL_OPTIONS, UI_INFO, NODE_STATES, COGNITION_INFO) into per-domain modules or typed slices
Split transient gesture state (DRAG_CONN) into a dedicated drag/gesture store
Split controller-installed action handles (CONNECT, FORCE_RUN) into an actions/actions-context module
Split NodeStateRender/NodeStateDef types and registry schemas into a types/constants file
Split generic external-store plumbing (subscribe/emit/getSnapshot) into a reusable factory
